copy.caption: Pink from head to toe, eating upside-down, standing on one leg, and completely unbothered.
copy.shortDescription: Flamingos are pink because of pigments in the algae and shrimp they eat — a flamingo fed plain white food slowly turns white. They filter-feed with their curved bill held upside-down, tongue pumping water through comb-like plates that trap tiny creatures. The one-leg stance might help conserve body heat, or it might just be comfortable — flamingos seem entirely at peace with the question. Fun fact: Baby flamingos are born grey and white, and the famous pink colour builds up slowly over two years of eating the right food.
